title: behavioral drift detection
manager: Turn statistical surprise into pre-outage incidents — compare live transition statistics against the machine's own Markov prediction, and alert when reality diverges from the model beyond a threshold. Drift detection is early warning: when a machine starts behaving differently than it provably should, that is a signal something upstream changed, caught before it becomes an outage rather than diagnosed in the postmortem after.
cs: drift = live transition stats vs §17 Markov prediction (χ² or KL threshold per edge); pre-outage early warning; feeds calibrate (W9.8).
